task: Menus, Party Management, Inventory + Save System
approach: Conservative — minimal changes to existing code; new files for new features; barrel exports for renderer splitting

files_to_create.description: New save system with 1|2|3 slots, SCHEMA_VERSION=1, SAVE_KEY='pokebrowser_save_v1', useStorageWarning React hook, save/load/listSlots/deleteSlot/isAvailable/autoSave. Distinct from existing engine/SaveSystem.ts (different storage key, richer API).
useItem(itemId, target, context), addItem, removeItem, getItemCount. Handles Potion/SuperPotion/FullRestore/Antidote/ParalyzHeal/FullHeal/Revive/Pokeball item effects.
renderStartMenu (slide-in from right, 5 items), renderSaveSlotScreen (3 slots), renderOptionsScreen, renderBadgeCase (2×4 grid). Uses Press Start 2P 8px font matching existing renderers.
renderPartyScreen: 6 portrait slots, HP bars color-coded green/yellow/red, rearrange mode support.
renderPokemonSummary: 64×64 sprite, name/level/types, HP bar, 4 moves with PP, base stats, OT/location.
renderBagScreen: category tabs, scrollable item list, USE/GIVE/TOSS/CANCEL popup, empty state.
renderPCScreen (6×5 grid), renderSavingAnimation (animated dots), renderToast (non-blocking message with alpha).
Barrel re-export file: re-exports all rendering functions from ui/ sub-renderers. Keeps import surface clean.

Vitest tests: round-trip save/load, slot independence, version check returns null, isAvailable false mock, autoSave calls save with correct slot. Located in tests/ (vitest config excludes e2e/** not tests/**).

changes: Add MenuPhase type and MenuState interface
Add menuState, inventory, partyPokemon, activeSlot fields to GameState
Update createInitialGameState() to include new fields with defaults
Add MENU phase case in update() — Enter key opens, x closes, arrow keys navigate
Add MENU case in render() — calls renderStartMenu + active sub-screen
Add item use flow connecting to InventorySystem
Import useStorageWarning hook from new SaveSystem
Render dismissible HTML overlay div when !available

steps: 2. Extend src/game/types/GameState.ts with new type definitions
12. Update app/page.tsx — add storage warning overlay
14. Run npm test — verify all tests pass
expected_tests: save/load round-trip: save GameState, load it, verify all fields match
slot independence: slot 1 and slot 2 data are independent
version mismatch: loading save with old schemaVersion returns null
isAvailable() returns false when localStorage.setItem throws
autoSave() calls save() with the active slot number
useItem Potion: +20 HP capped at maxHp, fails if already full or fainted
useItem Revive: fainted target revived at floor(maxHp/2)
useItem Pokeball: only valid in battle context, returns CATCH_ATTEMPT
addItem: immutably adds quantity to inventory
risk_areas: GameEngine.ts already exceeds 200 lines — menu additions will push it further. Plan: keep menu phase code compact, extract menu navigation to small helper function.
UIRenderer functions individually are large — mitigated by splitting into ui/ sub-files under 150 lines each.
The useStorageWarning hook imports React — verify the file compiles as a React module (add 'use client' comment if needed for Next.js).

New SaveSystem (systems/) uses SAVE_KEY='pokebrowser_save_v1' with slots 1|2|3, while existing SaveSystem (engine/) uses 'pokebrowser_save_0/1/2' with slots 0|1|2. They are completely separate; existing engine/ SaveSystem is NOT modified.

tests/ directory: vitest.config.ts excludes 'e2e/**' not 'tests/**', so tests/save-system.test.ts will be picked up by vitest. Playwright config may also scan tests/ — name the test file clearly as .test.ts (not .spec.ts) which is the vitest pattern.

persistence_strategy.fallback: isAvailable() returns false; game continues playable; useStorageWarning displays message
persistence_strategy.quota_handling: QuotaExceededError sets module-level saveAvailable=false with user-visible message

error_handling_approach.save_errors: QuotaExceededError and access errors caught; saveAvailable flag set false; useStorageWarning hook surfaces message
error_handling_approach.load_errors: JSON parse errors and schema version mismatch return null gracefully
error_handling_approach.item_errors: useItem returns {success: false, message: 'reason'} — never throws to calling code
error_handling_approach.inventory_errors: removeItem throws ItemInsufficientError for calling code to handle
